metadata.name: crossbar
metadata.description: Project CrossBar: frequent batch auction DEX clearing inside a MagicBlock Ephemeral Rollup

instructions.name: cancel_order
docs: F4: cancel, only while the order's window is still forming. Releases the
reserved escrow back to claimable once escrow is wired (M1).

instructions.name: commit
docs: F9: checkpoint canonical state to L1 every `commit_every_ticks`. Stamps
the commit slot so `force_undelegate` can measure the stall timeout.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Crank authority. `commit`/`undelegate_market` require this to equal
`market.crank_authority` (audit C2/H1); `force_undelegate` ignores it
(permissionless escape hatch) but still needs a signer present.

instructions.name: consume_marginal_vrf
docs: VRF callback: assign the marginal remainder to a tied order chosen by the
verifiable randomness. Only `vrf_winner` (the remainder recipient) moves;
`p*`, matched volume, and all non-marginal fills are untouched.

accounts.name: vrf_program_identity
docs: Enforces the callback is invoked by the VRF program via CPI.

instructions.name: consume_window_vrf
docs: VRF callback: set the forming window's close target to a value drawn
uniformly from `[window_min_ticks, window_max_ticks]`. Only the window
boundary moves; the matcher and `p*` are untouched (N1 preserved).

accounts.name: vrf_program_identity
docs: Enforces the callback is invoked by the VRF program via CPI.

instructions.name: delegate_market
docs: F2 / C6: delegate the PDAs `run_batch` writes into an ER session.
This delegates the `BatchBook` (the account `run_batch` mutates each
tick) following the verified `anchor-counter` pattern. The full
delegation set required by C6 ("Market + BatchBook + both Vaults,
together, before the first tick") is completed alongside the SPL escrow
wiring in M1; the per-account ergonomics follow `/magicblock`
delegation.md. Status is set to `Delegated` once the set is delegated.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Crank authority — must equal `market.crank_authority` (audit H2).

instructions.name: delegate_open_orders
docs: Delegate one trader's `OpenOrders` into the ER so `submit_order` /
`cancel_order` can update their reserved/claimable ledger inside the
rollup. Called per trader before they submit (`C6` for per-trader state).

accounts.name: authority
docs: Crank authority — must equal `market.crank_authority` (audit H2).

instructions.name: deposit
docs: Escrow deposit (L1): fund the trader's claimable balance with real SPL
tokens before delegation (`INTEGRATIONS.md` 5). `is_base` selects the
base or quote vault/mint.

instructions.name: finalize_settlement
docs: Flip the market back to `OnBase` once trader settlement is done
(crank-authority gated).

accounts.name: authority
docs: Checked against `market.crank_authority` in the handler.

instructions.name: force_undelegate
docs: F11: L1 escape hatch. After the stall timeout from the last commit
(`REQUIREMENTS.md` F-timeout), anyone may force the undelegation so
escrow is never stuck. The timeout is measured against `last_commit_slot`
on the base layer (gating, not matching, so N1 is unaffected). Before the
first commit, or when the timeout is disabled, the hatch is always open
(funds are still safe in L1 escrow).

accounts.name: authority
docs: Crank authority. `commit`/`undelegate_market` require this to equal
`market.crank_authority` (audit C2/H1); `force_undelegate` ignores it
(permissionless escape hatch) but still needs a signer present.

instructions.name: init_market
docs: F1: create the `Market`, `BatchBook`, and `BatchResult` PDAs on L1.
Vault pubkeys are recorded; the SPL escrow vaults themselves are wired
with `ephemeral-rollups-spl` in M1 (see `submit_order`).

accounts.name: base_vault
docs: Base custody vault, a PDA token account owned by the `Market` PDA.

accounts.name: quote_vault
docs: Quote custody vault.

instructions.name: make_open_orders_private
docs: PER: bind a permission to one trader's `OpenOrders` (their reserved
ledger) so their per-order amounts are TEE-confidential too, with the
crank authority as the reading member. Run before `delegate_open_orders`.

instructions.name: make_private
docs: PER (Private Ephemeral Rollup): make the market's clearing state private.
Binds a MagicBlock `permission` (live program
`ACLseoPoyC3cBqoUtkbjZ4aDrkurZW86v19pXz2XQnp1`) to the confidential
clearing accounts — `BatchBook` (resting order sizes) and `BatchResult`
(per-order fills) — listing the crank authority as the sole member with
full read access. Once these are delegated to a TEE validator and read via
the TEE RPC, only that member can decrypt the resting book, so a large
resting order no longer leaks a size signal across windows.
MUST run BEFORE `delegate_market` (the permission CPI requires the
permissioned account to sign, which only works while this program still
owns the PDA). `Market` config and the public `OraclePrice` reference are
intentionally left public. N1 is untouched: this changes *who can read*
the book, not the determinism of `run_batch`. See
`docs/integrations/PRIVATE_PAYMENTS.md` and `docs/N1_INVESTIGATION.md`.

instructions.name: request_marginal_vrf
docs: F6 (VRF tie-break, `MATH.md` section 4, `PLAN.md` T3.4): request
randomness from `ephemeral-vrf` to assign the indivisible marginal
remainder. VRF touches ONLY this remainder (a few base units), never
`p*` and never a non-marginal fill: its blast radius is deliberately
tiny because the library is unaudited. If VRF does not return in time,
the deterministic fallback already written by `run_batch` (lowest order
id) stands. No-op when there is no marginal remainder.

accounts.name: market
docs: Market for the crank-authority gate + result binding (audit R2-Low).

instructions.name: request_window_vrf
docs: F6 (randomized clearing time, `MATH.md` section 8.1): request VRF to set
the forming window's close target unpredictably within
`[window_min_ticks, window_max_ticks]`. Like the marginal tie-break, the
blast radius is bounded: if VRF does not return, the deterministic
fallback (`window_max_ticks`) closes the window. Reuses `ephemeral-vrf`.

instructions.name: run_batch
docs: F5/F6/F7: the heartbeat. Crank-only. Clears the current window at one
uniform `p*` via the pure matcher, writes `BatchResult`, then opens the
next window.
Determinism (N1): reads no clock or slot; the matcher is a pure function
of the order multiset and the selection rule. The oracle band and VRF
tie-break (M3) plug in here: the band gates accept/reject of `p*`, and
`ClearingRule::ReferenceClamped(p_ref)` replaces the default rule once
the Lazer read lands. Until then the default `MidpointFloor` is used.
CRANK AUTHORITY (`TECHNICALDESIGN.md` section 5): the only legitimate caller is
the registered crank schedule. The signer model of the scheduled task is
being reconciled against `/magicblock` cranks.md; the authority binding
(`Market.crank_authority`) is enforced once that is confirmed. Today the
guard is the lifecycle status check below.

accounts.name: oracle_price
docs: Reference price for the band gate. Read-only here; part of the delegated
set so the read is local to the ER.

instructions.name: schedule_batch
docs: Register the batch crank: schedule `run_batch` to fire every
`tick_interval_ms` inside the ER (`TECHNICALDESIGN.md` section 5). Modeled on the
verified `crank-counter` ScheduleTask CPI.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Crank authority — must equal `market.crank_authority` (audit C3).

instructions.name: set_delegated
docs: Mark the market delegated. Separated so it runs after the (possibly
multi-account) delegation set is in place. Kept on L1.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Checked against `market.crank_authority` in the handler.

instructions.name: settle
docs: F10 settlement (L1): reconcile ONE trader's ledger against the committed
`BatchResult`. Applies their fills at `p*` (buys gain base and spend
quote, sells give base and gain quote) and refunds the unspent escrow,
moving everything from `reserved` back to `claimable`. The trader then
pulls real tokens out with `withdraw`. Integer-only (`REQUIREMENTS.md`
C5); callable once per trader after `undelegate_market`.

instructions.name: submit_order
docs: F3: submit an order. Appends to the forming window and mirrors into the
trader's `OpenOrders`.
ESCROW (M1, `INTEGRATIONS.md` section 5): the max spend must be escrowed
into the matching `Vault` via `ephemeral-rollups-spl` before the order
is accepted. That CPI is wired against the vendored
`ephemeral-rollups-spl` API in M1; this handler currently records the
order and reserves the claimable accounting slot. It does NOT yet move
tokens, and must not be treated as custody-safe until escrow lands.

instructions.name: undelegate_market
docs: F10: commit and undelegate, returning canonical state to L1. Sets status
`Settling`. Per-trader token reconciliation happens in `settle`.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Crank authority. `commit`/`undelegate_market` require this to equal
`market.crank_authority` (audit C2/H1); `force_undelegate` ignores it
(permissionless escape hatch) but still needs a signer present.

instructions.name: undelegate_open_orders
docs: Commit + undelegate one trader's OpenOrders back to base so `settle` can
read it on L1 (ER -> base).

instructions.name: update_reference_price
docs: Push the latest Pyth Lazer reference price into `OraclePrice` (F8,
`TECHNICALDESIGN.md` section 7). Called by the oracle keeper / Lazer receiver.
`run_batch` reads this to bound `p*` and to detect a stale feed. Stamps
the current slot for the staleness check.

instructions.name: withdraw
docs: Escrow withdraw (L1): return claimable balance to the trader. Settled
fills become claimable in `settle`, then are withdrawn here.

errors.name: BatchFull
errors.msg: The forming batch window is at max_orders_per_batch

errors.name: WindowClosed
errors.msg: Cannot cancel: the order's batch window has already closed for clearing

errors.name: OutOfBand
errors.msg: Clearing price fell outside the Pyth Lazer reference band

errors.name: StaleOracle
errors.msg: Pyth Lazer feed is older than the configured max age; tick skipped

errors.name: NotCrankAuthority
errors.msg: run_batch may only be invoked by the configured crank authority

errors.name: WrongStatus
errors.msg: Market is not in the expected lifecycle status for this instruction

errors.name: VrfTimeout
errors.msg: VRF did not return in time; fell back to the deterministic tie-break

errors.name: MarketMismatch
errors.msg: Order references a market it does not belong to

errors.name: OrderNotFound
errors.msg: Order not found in the forming window

errors.name: InsufficientFunds
errors.msg: Insufficient deposited balance to escrow this order

errors.name: InvalidPermissionProgram
errors.msg: Permission-program account is not the canonical MagicBlock permission program

errors.name: AlreadySettled
errors.msg: This trader has already been settled for this batch window

errors.name: FillExceedsReserved
errors.msg: Filled quantity exceeds the trader's reserved escrow (matcher/escrow desync)

errors.name: OracleDeviation
errors.msg: Reference price update is zero or deviates too far from the previous price

fields.name: marginal_remainder
docs: Indivisible base units left at the margin after integer pro-rata, summed
across both sides. This (and only this) is the VRF blast radius.

fields.name: n_marginal_tied
docs: Order ids tied at the margin that share the remainder.

fields.name: vrf_winner
docs: The tied order assigned the remainder (set deterministically by
`run_batch`; overwritten by the VRF callback if it lands in time).

fields.name: side
docs: SIDE_BUY | SIDE_SELL (`book.rs`), so settlement is self-describing from
`BatchResult` alone without re-reading the book.

fields.name: tick_interval_ms
docs: Batch tick cadence (`TECHNICALDESIGN.md` section 3, default 50).

fields.name: commit_every_ticks
docs: Checkpoint cadence in ticks (`REQUIREMENTS.md` C7).

fields.name: band_delta_bps
docs: Reference-band half-width in basis points (`TECHNICALDESIGN.md` section 7). Zero
disables the band (clear without a reference check).

fields.name: oracle_max_age_slots
docs: Max age (in slots) of the oracle price before a tick is skipped as stale
(`REQUIREMENTS.md` oracle max-age). Zero disables the staleness check.

fields.name: max_orders_per_batch
docs: Slab capacity per batch; must match `BatchBook`/`BatchResult` capacity
and fit the `run_batch` CU budget (`REQUIREMENTS.md` C1, C2).

fields.name: crank_authority
docs: Only legitimate caller of `run_batch` (`TECHNICALDESIGN.md` section 5).

fields.name: current_window
docs: The forming batch window id; bumped each tick.

fields.name: next_order_id
docs: Monotonic order-id source.

fields.name: last_commit_slot
docs: Slot of the last `commit` checkpoint; drives the `force_undelegate`
stall timeout (`REQUIREMENTS.md` F-timeout).

fields.name: force_undelegate_timeout_slots
docs: After this many slots without a commit, anyone may `force_undelegate`
so escrow is never stuck (`REQUIREMENTS.md` F11). Zero disables.

fields.name: window_min_ticks
docs: Randomized clearing-time band, in crank ticks (`MATH.md` section 8.1,
arXiv 2405.09764). The window closes after `window_target_ticks` crank
ticks; the target is drawn from `[window_min_ticks, window_max_ticks]`
by VRF and is unpredictable until the window closes. `window_max_ticks
<= 1` disables randomization (close every tick, original cadence).

fields.name: window_target_ticks
docs: Current window's close target (set to max as a deterministic fallback at
window open; re-randomized into the band by `consume_window_vrf`).

fields.name: window_ticks_elapsed
docs: Crank ticks elapsed in the forming window.

fields.name: cfmm_base
docs: Constant-product CFMM backstop reserves (`MATH.md` section 8.3, arXiv
2210.04929). When both are non-zero, `run_batch` adds the pool's
synthetic maker ladder to the batch so a thin book still clears against
passive liquidity. `cfmm_band_bps` is the ladder half-width around spot;
`cfmm_levels` is the discretization per side.

fields.name: delegated_at_slot
docs: Slot at which the market was last delegated (set in `set_delegated`).
Used as the `force_undelegate` stall baseline before the first commit so
a freshly-delegated market cannot be torn down instantly (audit M3).

types.name: MarketStatus
docs: Lifecycle status. Encoded as a Borsh enum on this normal (non zero-copy)
account, so a real enum is fine here (`REQUIREMENTS.md` C4 only constrains
the zero-copy accounts).

fields.name: base_claimable
docs: Free balance the trader has deposited and can spend or withdraw.
Credited by `deposit` (L1) and by clearing fills; debited by `withdraw`
(L1) and when an order is submitted (moved to `*_reserved`).

fields.name: base_reserved
docs: Balance locked behind live orders (the escrow). Moved here from
claimable on `submit_order`, back on `cancel_order`, and converted at
`p*` on settlement. This is the internal escrow ledger that lets
`submit_order` run inside the ER with no token CPI (the real SPL moves
happen only at the L1 boundary, `deposit`/`withdraw`).

fields.name: last_settled_window
docs: Window id of the last `BatchResult` this trader was settled against
(`u64::MAX` = never settled). Makes `settle` one-shot per (trader, window)
so fills cannot be re-credited and the vault drained (audit C1).

fields.name: authority
docs: Who may push prices (the Lazer keeper / receiver authority).

fields.name: price
docs: Reference price scaled to `PRICE_SCALE`. Zero means "unset": the band is
treated as disabled until a real price is pushed (so a market can run
before the oracle is wired, e.g. in the demo).

fields.name: last_update_slot
docs: Slot at which `price` was last written; used for the staleness check.
